name: Muhammad ibn Abi Said
title: Terrified Child
shortDescription: The young boy of Aqeel's family who ran out of the tent in terror after Imam Hussain's (AS) martyrdom and was killed by enemy soldiers.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn Abi Sa'id ibn Aqeel ibn Abi Talib was one of the young children (or early teenagers) of the brave family of Aqeel. He was present in the tents with his mother and other women.

On the afternoon of Ashura, after Imam Hussain (AS) was martyred and the cries of women rose from the tents, Muhammad, who was a young child (about 7 or 10 years old), grabbed a tent peg and ran outside with terrified and anxious eyes to see what had happened to his uncle.

At that moment, one of the hard-hearted enemy soldiers named 'Laqit ibn Iyas al-Juhani' (or Hani ibn Thubayt) attacked this defenseless child and struck him with a sword, unjustly martyring him.

This bitter scene, which occurred in front of his weeping mother's eyes, is considered one of the most painful pages of the Kufan army's crimes on the afternoon of Ashura.
quoteArabic: خَرَجَ غُلامٌ مِنْ عَسْكَرِ الحُسَيْنِ وَفِي أُذُنَيْهِ دُرَّتانِ... وَهُوَ مَذْعُورٌ يَلْتَفِتُ يَمِيناً وَشِمالاً...
quote: (Narrated by Hani ibn Thubayt:) A boy with two pearl earrings came out of the tent, looking right and left in terror...
